milestone.status: generic JSON submit wrapper expanded into adjacent lifecycle/update pipeline
milestone.meaning: 0x1400ED4CC is not a standalone endpoint-specific caller. It is the submit half of a stateful object update pipeline split across adjacent .pdata records, with endpoint/body values supplied through object/string state rather than embedded endpoint literals.

contiguous_ranges.role: transient request-state allocation, child state copying, and body/source preparation
evidence: Allocates 0x2C8 bytes via ECX=0x2C8 before CALL 0x140793B40.
Initializes request-state fields at +0x268 through +0x2C0 to zero.
Uses RDI+0x110 as a child/source pointer and cleans or copies child string slots such as +0x268, +0x280, +0x298, and +0x2B0.
Calls 0x1400EDC40 with RCX=RDI and RDX=[RBP-0x70] before string comparison and submit gating.

contiguous_ranges.role: dirty-state gate and cached string boundary commit
evidence: Calls 0x1400EEAB0 using RDI+0x30, local string state at [RBP-0x70], and child/state data around +0x268.
Branches into 0x1400ED4CC when cached/local string boundary pairs differ.
Branches into 0x1400ED4CC when the RDI+0xC0 dirty flag is nonzero.
When clean, commits RDI+0xC8, RDI+0xD0, and RDI+0xD8 boundary/state fields before cleanup.

contiguous_ranges.role: JSON header setup, shared submitter call, post-submit callback dispatch, and cleanup
evidence: Only embedded literals are Content-Type and application/json.
At 0x1400ED700, RCX is the request object loaded from [R15], RDX is an endpoint/source pointer loaded through memory, and R8 is a small-string-selected JSON/body string at [RSP+0x68].
Calls the shared OTL submitter 0x14062BCE0 after appending JSON headers.
After submit, optionally dispatches through global 0x140E57B40 via object slot +0x198 and vtable slot +0x20.
Sets RDI+0x10 to 3 and clears RDI+0xC0 during post-submit state update.

caller_trace.implication: The pipeline is probably entered from an indirect callback/table path or a parent control-flow edge not represented as a direct CALL to these .pdata starts. Next xref work should target object/vtable registration and the global callback dispatcher rather than another direct-call scan.

opennxt_947_implications: This request family should be modeled as a stateful JSON update pipeline: endpoint and body are supplied by object/string state, not by literals in the submit wrapper.
The client compares and caches multiple small-string fields before submitting, so repeated identical state may skip the JSON request.
The post-submit callback through global 0x140E57B40 suggests async completion or UI/service notification behavior after the HTTP submitter returns.
next_steps: Trace registrations or writes for global 0x140E57B40 and dispatcher object fields +0x198, +0x1A0, +0x1A8, and +0x1F8.
Trace how RDI is seeded for 0x1400ED144/0x1400ED3A7 so the updater object can be named.
Correlate 0x1400EDC40 with the +0x268 string family to name the JSON body fields carried by this generic wrapper.
